summary: Phase 2 LithoForge marketplace merge retest of iter-121 HIGH-priority /litho/payouts loading bug. Auth shim fix at /app/frontend/src/components/litho/lib/auth.jsx (re-export of ForgeSlicer's global AuthContext) works as intended. All 6 requested frontend flows PASS. Backend regression not re-run (no backend changes since iter-121; shim is FE-only).
critical_code_review_comments: auth.jsx shim is well-structured: proxies useAuth to global provider with defensive no-op fallbacks for login/logout/refresh. AuthCallbackHandler is a no-op (correctly prevents double-fire). AuthProvider passthrough preserves import-compat.

One transient 401 on /api/auth/me on first paint is observed on hard-navigation to /litho/payouts. It self-corrects on the follow-up /me call and does not block rendering — the AuthProvider effect re-fires and populates `user`. This is pre-existing behaviour (also present in iter-121). Not a regression from the shim.

Non-blocking: iter-121's medium-priority findings (checkout vs checkout-bt naming, missing /checkout/status/{session_id}) are unchanged. Main agent already flagged these as documentation / dead-code, not real bugs.

success_rate.backend: not-retested (no BE changes since iter-121, previous 100%)

test_credentials: session_token=st_test_litho_1783361464350 (still valid; user_test_litho_1783361464350). Cookie domain orca-cad-slice.preview.emergentagent.com, secure=True, httpOnly=True, sameSite=None. Bearer token also works.
seed_data_creation: None new. Seeded listing cc3bb001-251a-48c3-a671-d242ac78f270 ($4.99 Sunset Gradient) still exists. Two seeded transactions ($4.99 each, payouts pending, buyer=buyer@test.com) already present. Test 2 saved paypal.retest.1783369267@example.com to the seeded user (previously paypal.test.ad18d8@example.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-121 HIGH-priority /litho/payouts bug fully resolved. Auth shim pattern at /app/frontend/src/components/litho/lib/auth.jsx re-exports @/contexts/AuthContext. All LithoForge components importing `../lib/auth` or `../../lib/auth` now transparently read from ForgeSlicer's global AuthProvider — verified via /litho/payouts (PayoutsPage), /litho (JobHistory/PresetManager/LibraryMatchPanel), /litho/marketplace (ListingCard), /litho/marketplace/{jobId} (ListingDetailPage + Buy/PurchaseDialog). No other regressions observed. Backend suite (34/34 iter-121) does not need re-run — shim is FE-only.
rca of the issue: Iter-121 root cause: PayoutsPage.jsx did `import { useAuth } from '../../lib/auth'`, resolving to `/app/frontend/src/components/litho/lib/auth.js` — a self-contained AuthContext whose Provider was never mounted at App root. `useAuth()` returned the createContext default `{user:null, loading:true}` so the `if (authLoading) return <Loading… />` guard short-circuited forever. Iter-122 fix (verified): main agent renamed auth.js → auth.jsx and rewrote it as a compatibility shim — `useAuth` now proxies through to `@/contexts/AuthContext` (which IS mounted at index.js). All downstream LithoForge consumers of the same import path (PayoutsPage, JobHistory, PresetManager, LibraryMatchPanel, FilamentLibraryDialog, PurchaseDialog, quota.jsx) transparently get the real signed-in user. Playwright verified across /litho/payouts, /litho, /litho/marketplace, /litho/marketplace/{jobId}, /workspace with the seeded session cookie.

Seeded values populated correctly: paypal_email=paypal.test.ad18d8@example.com, pending=$9.38, threshold=$1.00, mode=mock.
